panels.title: Search Stream Latency (over time)
panels.description: Histogram-backed server-side gRPC stream latency for search requests. This is closer to the end-to-end trace duration than the in-command search timer.

panels.title: Search Stream Latency (heatmap)
panels.description: Built from Prometheus histogram buckets emitted by searchCommandStreamLatency, using Grafana's rate interval so bucket counts align with the 15s scrape cadence.

targets.expr: sum by (le) (increase(mongot_command_searchCommandStreamLatency_seconds_bucket{timeUnit="seconds"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Search Requests / sec
panels.description: Success is derived from total search commands minus invalid queries, internal search failures, and command failures.

(sum(rate(mongot_search_metrics_searchCommandInvalidQueries_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(mongot_search_metrics_searchCommandInternalFailures_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(mongot_command_searchCommandFailure_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0))

panels.title: Search Stream Breakdown (mean)
panels.description: Stacked mean latency for the MongoT search stream. Child spans are shown with non-overlapping residual buckets, and the stream lifecycle bucket is the measured stream latency minus the MongoT command span. Queries explicitly add the corrected metric and the earlier accidental double-prefixed metric so Prometheus range queries do not create duplicate labelsets.
